name: Garlic Butter
recipeIngredient: 1/2 Cup lightly salted butter, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, cream the butter with a wooden spoon until light and fluffy.
2. Add the garlic and blend well.
3. Transfer the mixture to a sheet of heavy-duty foil.
4. Roll the foil around the mixture, forming a log that is 8 inches long by 3/4 inch in diameter.
5. Tuck in the ends of the foil.
6. Label the butter and freeze until ready to use.
7. The frozen garlic butter will keep for up to 2 months.
8. Use it to flavor potatoes, bread, other cooked vegetables, rice pasta, and pasta sauces.
I like to keep these "butters" on hand in the freezer for special occasions, such as when company is over or during holiday meals. Use them to flavor rice and vegetables or spread them on bread. A 1/4 inch slice equals 1 teaspoon, and a 3/4 inch slice equals 1 tablespoon. To make decorative twists, place the butter in a pastry bag, roll it out on a jellyroll pan, cover with plastic wrap, freeze until hard, then transfer to a gallon-sized freezer bag. Alternatively, simply roll the butter into a log and freeze that way.
